sections.paragraph: (“black skin,” “black skinned man,”) [Kedar], pr.n. of a son of Ishmael, Gen 25:13 and of an Arabian tribe sprung from him, Son 1:5 Isa 42:11 (where it is joined with a fem.); 60:7 Jer 49:28 Eze 27:21 more fully called בְּנֵי קֵדָר Isa 21:17 by Pliny (H. N. 5:11), Cedrei. The Rabbins call all the Arabians universally by this name; whence לשון קדר Rabbin. used of the Arabic language.
